MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-24-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, no appearances, and tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Samuel Turner's Motion for an Order for Leave to file a First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed. The Court intends to execute Plaintiffs' proposed order.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Not Reported. (304)

ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE

Off Calendar Jun-06-2025 Off Calendar - Assigned To Trial

PLAINTIFF SAMUEL TURNER, RUBYE TURNER NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY.)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2)
(Tentative ruling continues from previous entry.)

8. No later than March 11, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.

9. A further case management conference is set for April 29, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than May 5, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due May 19, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on May 27, 2025. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 12, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Court to review the proposed order submitted by counsel for Plaintiff.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. (304/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The trial date is June 16, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 25, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 10, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is May 30, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is June 13, 2025.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 27, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini minutes continue, see next entry) (Part 1 of 2)
